metadata.description: Comprehensive career dataset for AI-based career recommendation, skill gap analysis, and roadmap generation

careers.description: A licensed financial expert responsible for auditing, taxation, financial reporting, and advisory services for individuals, firms, and corporations.
education_required: 10+2 in any stream (Commerce preferred)
CA Foundation (entry route post-12th)
CA Intermediate (both groups)
3-year Articleship under a practicing CA
CA Final (both groups)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: ICAI registration mandatory. Graduates can enter via CA Direct Entry route skipping Foundation. All three levels (Foundation, Intermediate, Final) must be cleared through ICAI.
certifications: Certificate Course on Forensic Accounting and Fraud Detection
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in Commerce (or any stream)
Register with ICAI for CA Foundation
Clear CA Foundation exam (4 papers)
Register and clear CA Intermediate Group I and Group II
Begin 3-year Articleship under a registered CA firm
Complete Advanced IT Training and Management Training
Clear CA Final Group I and Group II during or after articleship
Obtain ICAI membership and Certificate of Practice (if practicing)
Choose specialization: Audit, Tax, Corporate Finance, or Advisory
careers.experience_level: Entry (Articleship) → Junior CA → Senior CA → Partner
careers.average_salary_range: INR 6–12 LPA (fresher); INR 15–40 LPA (5+ years); INR 50 LPA+ (Partner level)
careers.future_scope: High demand in Big 4 firms (Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG), corporate finance, CFO roles, government audits, and international practice with ACCA/CPA equivalency.

careers.description: A corporate governance professional responsible for legal compliance, board advisory, secretarial functions, and ensuring adherence to corporate laws and regulations.
required_skills: Board meeting management and minute drafting
education_required: 10+2 in any stream
CS Foundation Program (ICSI)
CS Executive Program
CS Professional Program
15-month practical training
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Graduates can directly enroll in CS Executive without clearing CS Foundation. Membership of ICSI is mandatory to practice as CS. LLB is an added advantage but not mandatory.
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream
Register for CS Foundation with ICSI
Clear CS Foundation exam (4 papers)
Enroll in CS Executive Program and clear both modules
Enroll in CS Professional Program and clear all modules
Complete 15-month mandatory practical training
Obtain ICSI Associate Membership (ACS)
Apply for Certificate of Practice (optional, for independent practice)
Progress to Fellow Membership (FCS) after 5 years of experience
careers.experience_level: Trainee → Junior CS → Company Secretary → Group CS → Independent Practice
careers.average_salary_range: INR 5–9 LPA (fresher); INR 12–25 LPA (5+ years); INR 30–60 LPA (listed companies/senior roles)
careers.future_scope: Mandatory role in all listed and public companies. Growing demand due to increasing compliance requirements, SEBI regulations, IBC proceedings, and ESG governance mandates.

careers.description: A finance professional who helps organizations raise capital, execute mergers and acquisitions, manage IPOs, and provide strategic financial advisory services.
required_skills: Financial modeling and valuation (DCF, LBO, precedent transactions)
education_required: Bachelor's in Finance, Economics, Commerce, or Engineering
MBA in Finance (highly preferred for senior roles)
CFA (CFA Institute) — strongly recommended
careers.specific_degree_requirements: MBA from a top-tier institution (IIM, ISB, XLRI) is near-essential for front-office investment banking roles in India. Engineering graduates from IITs often enter via lateral hiring or top MBA programs.
certifications: CFA Level I, II, III (CFA Institute)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in Commerce or Science
Pursue B.Com/BBA/B.Tech (Economics or Finance focus preferred)
Gain internship at a financial firm during undergraduate
Pursue MBA in Finance from a reputed institution OR directly enter as analyst via campus placement
Join as Investment Banking Analyst (IBA) at a bank or boutique firm
Progress to Associate, then Vice President (VP) level
Pursue CFA charter alongside career
Advance to Director and Managing Director (MD) roles
Specialize in sector: Healthcare, Tech, Real Estate, or Infrastructure banking
careers.experience_level: Analyst → Associate → VP → Director → Managing Director
careers.average_salary_range: INR 10–18 LPA (analyst); INR 25–60 LPA (associate/VP); INR 80 LPA–2 Cr+ (MD level with bonuses)
careers.future_scope: Robust growth driven by India's increasing IPO activity, cross-border M&A, infrastructure investments, and startup ecosystem. Global mobility is high for CFA/MBA holders.

careers.description: Analyzes financial data, prepares reports, evaluates investment opportunities, and provides recommendations to help businesses and investors make informed financial decisions.
required_skills: Ratio analysis and financial statement interpretation
education_required: Bachelor's in Finance, Economics, Commerce, or Statistics
MBA in Finance (preferred for senior roles)
CFA designation (highly valued)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Com/BBA with finance electives as minimum. CFA is internationally recognized and significantly boosts career prospects in equity research, asset management, and portfolio analysis.
certifications: CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) — Level I, II, III
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in Commerce or Science (Math mandatory)
Pursue B.Com/BBA/BSc Economics
Gain internship in equity research, banking, or corporate finance
Start as Junior Financial Analyst
Pursue CFA Level I while working
Progress to Senior Analyst with specialization (equity/credit/FP&A)
Obtain full CFA charter
Move to portfolio management, research head, or CFO track
careers.experience_level: Junior Analyst → Senior Analyst → Lead Analyst → Research Head / Portfolio Manager
careers.average_salary_range: INR 5–10 LPA (entry); INR 15–30 LPA (3–6 years); INR 40–80 LPA (senior/fund management)
careers.future_scope: High demand across mutual funds, hedge funds, corporate finance, FP&A, and fintech. Increasing automation is shifting roles toward interpretation, strategy, and AI-driven analytics.

careers.description: Manages banking operations including customer accounts, credit appraisal, loan processing, branch management, and regulatory compliance for public or private sector banks.
education_required: Bachelor's degree in any discipline (minimum for clerk/PO level)
MBA in Banking & Finance (for specialist/officer roles in private banks)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: PSU banks (SBI, PNB, etc.) recruit via IBPS/SBI exams open to any graduate. Private banks (HDFC, ICICI) may prefer MBA or finance graduates for officer-track roles.
certifications: JAIIB (Junior Associate of Indian Institute of Bankers)
CAIIB (Certified Associate of Indian Institute of Bankers)
career_path_steps: Complete graduation in any stream
Prepare for IBPS/SBI exams (Prelims + Mains + Interview)
Join as Probationary Officer (PO) or Clerk
Complete induction training at the bank's training institute
Clear JAIIB and CAIIB certifications
Progress to Branch Manager → Regional Manager
Pursue RBI Grade B for central banking / regulatory career
Specialize in credit, forex, treasury, or wealth management
careers.experience_level: Clerk/PO (Entry) → Officer → Branch Manager → Zonal Manager → General Manager
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3.5–6 LPA (clerk/PO entry); INR 8–15 LPA (branch manager); INR 20–40 LPA (GM/DGM level)
careers.future_scope: India's banking expansion, financial inclusion drives, and digital banking transformation ensure sustained demand. RBI Grade B and NABARD offer regulatory and development finance career tracks.

careers.description: Manages recruitment, employee relations, performance management, training and development, payroll, and HR policy compliance within an organization.
education_required: BBA/B.Com/BA (any discipline for entry level)
MBA in Human Resource Management (preferred for managerial roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: MBA-HR from a recognized institution is the standard pathway for managerial HR roles. Postgraduate Diploma in Personnel Management (PGDPM) is an alternative.
certifications: SHRM-CP or SHRM-SCP (Society for Human Resource Management)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th and pursue BBA/BA/B.Com
Pursue MBA in HRM from a recognized institution
Start as HR Executive or HR Generalist
Gain experience in recruitment, payroll, and employee relations
Progress to HR Manager handling full employee lifecycle
Specialize: Talent Acquisition, L&D, HRBP, or Compensation & Benefits
Advance to HR Business Partner (HRBP) or HR Head
Move toward Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO)
careers.experience_level: HR Executive → HR Manager → Senior HR Manager → HRBP → HR Head → CHRO
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry); INR 8–18 LPA (manager); INR 25–60 LPA (CHRO/HR Director)
careers.future_scope: Growing demand for HR tech expertise, people analytics, remote work management, DEI (Diversity, Equity, Inclusion) roles, and organizational design in global MNCs and startups.

careers.description: Extracts actionable insights from large datasets using statistical modeling, machine learning, and programming. Builds predictive models to support business decisions across industries.
education_required: Bachelor's in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or Engineering
Master's in Data Science, Statistics, or AI/ML (preferred for senior roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Tech/B.Sc in CS, Math, or Statistics is the most common entry route. Postgraduate degrees (M.Tech, M.Sc, MS) in Data Science or AI significantly improve placement prospects at top firms.
entrance_exams: JEE Main/Advanced (for B.Tech CS at IITs/NITs)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with Math and Science
Pursue B.Tech CS / B.Sc Statistics or Mathematics
Learn Python, SQL, and ML fundamentals during undergrad
Build a portfolio of 3–5 projects on GitHub
Complete internship as Data Analyst or ML Intern
Start as Junior Data Scientist or Data Analyst
Pursue M.Sc/M.Tech Data Science or industry certifications
Advance to Senior Data Scientist or ML Engineer
Lead a data science team or transition to AI Product/Research
careers.experience_level: Data Analyst → Junior Data Scientist → Data Scientist → Senior DS → Lead/Principal DS → Head of Data
careers.average_salary_range: INR 6–12 LPA (entry); INR 18–35 LPA (3–5 years); INR 40–80 LPA+ (senior/lead at top tech firms)
careers.future_scope: One of the fastest-growing roles globally. Increasing applications in healthcare AI, climate tech, fintech, autonomous systems, and GenAI products. Demand far exceeds supply in India.

careers.description: Designs, develops, tests, and maintains software applications, systems, and platforms using programming languages and software development methodologies.
required_skills: Proficiency in at least one programming language (Java, Python, C++, JavaScript)
education_required: B.Tech/B.E. in Computer Science, IT, or Electronics
B.Sc Computer Science (alternate route)
M.Tech CS (for research or senior roles at product companies)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Tech in Computer Science from NIT/IIT or a reputed private college is the primary route. Non-CS engineers or BCA/B.Sc graduates can transition via coding bootcamps or online courses.
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with Physics, Chemistry, Math (PCM)
Clear JEE or State CET for B.Tech admission
Learn core CS fundamentals: DSA, OOP, DBMS, OS
Build projects and contribute to open source
Complete software development internship
Join as Software Engineer I (entry level)
Progress to Software Engineer II → Senior Software Engineer
Specialize: Backend, Frontend, Full-Stack, DevOps, or Embedded
Move toward Principal Engineer, Tech Lead, or Engineering Manager
careers.experience_level: Intern → SWE I → SWE II → Senior SWE → Staff/Principal Engineer → Engineering Manager → Director of Engineering
careers.average_salary_range: INR 5–12 LPA (fresher); INR 20–40 LPA (3–6 years); INR 50–150 LPA+ (senior/FAANG-level)
careers.future_scope: Continuously evolving role with demand in AI/ML, cloud computing, Web3, cybersecurity, and quantum computing. Remote work opportunities and global job markets are widely accessible.

careers.description: Protects organizational networks, systems, and data from cyber threats. Monitors security infrastructure, detects vulnerabilities, responds to incidents, and implements security policies.
education_required: B.Tech in CS, IT, or Electronics
B.Sc Computer Science (with cybersecurity electives)
M.Tech in Cybersecurity or Information Security (preferred for senior roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: No single mandatory degree, but B.Tech CS or IT is the most common entry path. Industry certifications carry equal or greater weight than degrees in this domain.
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM
Pursue B.Tech in CS/IT or B.Sc CS
Build foundational knowledge in networking (CompTIA Network+)
Earn CompTIA Security+ certification
Practice on platforms: HackTheBox, TryHackMe, PicoCTF
Gain entry-level role: SOC Analyst Tier 1 or Security Intern
Earn CEH and OSCP certifications
Advance to Penetration Tester, Security Engineer, or Threat Intelligence Analyst
Pursue CISSP/CISM for senior governance and management roles
Lead as CISO (Chief Information Security Officer)
careers.experience_level: SOC Analyst Tier 1 → Tier 2 Analyst → Penetration Tester → Security Engineer → Security Architect → CISO
careers.average_salary_range: INR 5–10 LPA (entry); INR 15–30 LPA (3–5 years); INR 40–80 LPA (CISO/senior architect)
careers.future_scope: Critical shortage of cybersecurity professionals globally. India's growing digital economy, government mandates (DPDP Act), and fintech expansion make this one of the highest-demand roles for the next decade.

careers.description: Develops and deploys AI/ML models and systems including neural networks, NLP models, computer vision systems, and recommendation engines for production environments.
required_skills: MLOps and model deployment (Docker, Kubernetes, MLflow)
Cloud ML services (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI)
education_required: B.Tech in CS, Mathematics, or Statistics
M.Tech or M.Sc in AI/ML, Data Science, or CS (strongly preferred)
PhD (for research-oriented roles at AI labs)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Strong mathematical foundation is essential. M.Tech or MS from top institutions (IITs, IISc, CMU, Stanford) significantly enhances prospects for top AI roles. Research publications are valued at product AI labs.
entrance_exams: GATE CS/DA (Data Science paper for M.Tech)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM (strong Math background)
Pursue B.Tech CS or B.Sc Math/Statistics
Master Python, linear algebra, calculus, and probability
Complete Deep Learning Specialization and hands-on projects
Build NLP or computer vision portfolio projects
Intern as ML Research Intern or AI Engineer Intern
Join as Junior ML Engineer or AI Researcher
Pursue M.Tech AI or contribute to open-source AI projects
Advance to Senior ML Engineer → ML Lead → AI Architect
Contribute to foundation model development or pursue PhD for AI Research Scientist roles
careers.experience_level: ML Intern → Junior ML Engineer → ML Engineer → Senior ML Engineer → Staff ML Engineer → Principal AI Researcher
careers.average_salary_range: INR 8–15 LPA (entry); INR 25–60 LPA (3–6 years); INR 80 LPA–2 Cr+ (senior at top AI firms/FAANG)
careers.future_scope: Explosive growth driven by generative AI, large language models, autonomous systems, healthcare AI, and climate modeling. Among the top 3 most in-demand technical roles globally through 2030.

careers.description: Plans, designs, constructs, and oversees infrastructure projects including roads, bridges, buildings, dams, water supply systems, and urban development projects.
education_required: B.Tech/B.E. in Civil Engineering
M.Tech in Structural Engineering, Transportation, or Environmental Engineering (for specialization)
PhD (for academic/research roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Tech Civil Engineering from an AICTE-approved institution is the minimum. PE (Professional Engineer) license or institution membership (MIE) is valued for senior roles. GATE is required for PSU and government roles.
certifications: Member of Institution of Engineers India (MIE)
entrance_exams: GATE Civil Engineering (for M.Tech, PSU jobs)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM
Clear JEE / State CET and join B.Tech Civil Engineering
Learn AutoCAD, STAAD Pro, and structural design during undergrad
Complete internship at a construction firm or consulting company
Join as Junior Civil Engineer or Site Engineer
Clear GATE for PSU roles (NHAI, NTPC, CPWD) or pursue M.Tech
Progress to Project Manager → Senior Project Manager
Pursue PMP certification for leadership roles
Advance to Project Director or start independent consultancy
careers.experience_level: Junior Engineer → Civil Engineer → Senior Engineer → Project Manager → Project Director → VP Projects
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry); INR 8–20 LPA (5 years); INR 25–60 LPA (senior/PM level); PSU: INR 5–15 LPA
careers.future_scope: Strong government investment in infrastructure (PM GatiShakti, Smart Cities, AMRUT), real estate growth, metro rail expansion, and increasing adoption of BIM and smart construction technologies.

careers.description: Designs, develops, tests, and manufactures mechanical systems, machinery, automotive components, HVAC systems, robotics, and industrial equipment.
education_required: B.Tech/B.E. in Mechanical Engineering
M.Tech in Thermal, Manufacturing, Design, or Automotive Engineering
PhD (for academic/R&D roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Tech Mechanical from AICTE-approved or NITs/IITs is the primary path. Specialization in robotics, EV powertrain, or aerospace significantly boosts career prospects.
certifications: Six Sigma Green Belt / Black Belt
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM
Pursue B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering
Learn SolidWorks / CATIA and thermodynamics during undergrad
Complete internship in an automotive, aerospace, or manufacturing firm
Join as Graduate Engineer Trainee or Design Engineer
Clear GATE for PSU roles (BHEL, ONGC, HAL) or pursue M.Tech
Gain experience in design, manufacturing, or maintenance
Progress to Senior Engineer → Technical Lead → Engineering Manager
Specialize in EV, robotics, or renewable energy systems
careers.experience_level: Trainee → Design/Manufacturing Engineer → Senior Engineer → Lead Engineer → Engineering Manager → VP Engineering
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry); INR 8–18 LPA (5 years); INR 20–50 LPA (senior/managerial roles)
careers.future_scope: Growing demand in EV manufacturing, aerospace, defense, robotics and automation, hydrogen energy, and smart factories (Industry 4.0). Core to India's Make in India initiative.

careers.description: Designs, develops, and maintains electrical systems, power infrastructure, control systems, and electronic devices across industries including power utilities, manufacturing, and telecoms.
education_required: B.Tech/B.E. in Electrical or Electrical & Electronics Engineering
M.Tech in Power Systems, Control Systems, or Embedded Systems
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Tech EE from accredited institution. GATE is important for PSU recruitment (NTPC, PGCIL, BHEL). Renewable energy and smart grid specializations are increasingly relevant.
certifications: Certified Energy Auditor (BEE — Bureau of Energy Efficiency)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM
Pursue B.Tech in Electrical Engineering
Learn circuit theory, power systems, and MATLAB
Intern at a power company, substation, or manufacturing plant
Join as Electrical Engineer / Graduate Engineer Trainee
Clear GATE for PSU roles (NTPC, PGCIL, BHEL, BPCL)
Advance to Senior Electrical Engineer → Project Manager
Specialize in renewables, smart grids, or power electronics
tools_or_technologies: PLC Software (Siemens TIA Portal, Allen-Bradley Studio 5000)
careers.experience_level: Trainee → Electrical Engineer → Senior Engineer → Project Manager → Chief Electrical Engineer
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3.5–7 LPA (entry); INR 8–20 LPA (5 years); INR 15–40 LPA PSU scale with DAs (senior level)
careers.future_scope: Critical for India's power sector expansion, renewable energy transition (solar, wind), EV charging infrastructure, and smart grid modernization.

careers.description: A licensed medical professional who diagnoses, treats, and manages patient health across various specialties including general medicine, surgery, pediatrics, and others.
required_skills: Medical history taking and physical examination
education_required: 12th with Physics, Chemistry, Biology (PCB)
MBBS (5.5 years including 1-year internship)
MD/MS (3-year postgraduate specialization — optional but required for specialists)
DM/MCh (super-specialization — 3 years, for fields like Cardiology, Neurology)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: NEET-UG is mandatory for MBBS admission in India. NEET-PG is required for MD/MS admissions. NEET-SS for super-specialty (DM/MCh). MCI/NMC registration is compulsory to practice.
certifications: Subspecialty Diploma (DNB, Diploma in Cardiology, etc.)
entrance_exams: FMGE (for foreign medical graduates practicing in India)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCB (minimum 50% for NEET eligibility)
Prepare for and clear NEET-UG
Enroll in MBBS (4.5 years academic + 1 year compulsory rotating internship)
Obtain provisional NMC registration after internship
Prepare for NEET-PG and choose specialty (MD/MS/DNB)
Complete 3-year MD/MS residency
Optional: Clear NEET-SS for super-specialty (DM/MCh — 3 more years)
Register with State Medical Council and begin practice
Join as Consultant, RMO, or open private practice
careers.experience_level: Medical Student → MBBS Intern → Junior Resident → Senior Resident (MD) → Consultant/Specialist
careers.average_salary_range: INR 6–10 LPA (junior doctor/RMO); INR 15–40 LPA (MBBS consultant); INR 30–150 LPA+ (senior specialist/superspecialist)
careers.future_scope: Perpetually high demand, especially in Tier 2/3 cities. Telemedicine growth, AI-assisted diagnostics, and health tech integration are transforming clinical practice. India faces a severe doctor shortage.

careers.description: Specializes in medicinal drugs, their formulation, dispensing, safety, and therapeutic use. Works in retail pharmacies, hospitals, pharmaceutical companies, and drug regulatory agencies.
education_required: D.Pharm (2-year diploma for entry-level dispensing)
B.Pharm (4-year degree — preferred)
M.Pharm (2-year PG for specialization)
Pharm.D (6-year integrated clinical pharmacy)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) registration mandatory for all pharmacists. B.Pharm is the industry standard for pharmaceutical company roles. Pharm.D for clinical pharmacy practice in hospitals.
entrance_exams: State-level pharmacy entrance exams (WBJEE, KCET, etc.)
GPAT — Graduate Pharmacy Aptitude Test (for M.Pharm admission)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCB or PCM
Pursue B.Pharm or Pharm.D
Register with State Pharmacy Council
Start as Retail/Hospital Pharmacist or Pharma Sales Rep
Pursue M.Pharm for specialization (Pharmaceutics, Pharmacology, Clinical Pharmacy)
Move to pharmaceutical R&D, regulatory affairs, or clinical research
Advance to Senior Pharmacist → Drug Inspector → R&D Lead
Clear GPAT for M.Pharm or fellowship programs
careers.experience_level: Junior Pharmacist → Senior Pharmacist → Chief Pharmacist → Drug Inspector / Regulatory Affairs Manager
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2.5–5 LPA (D.Pharm/B.Pharm entry); INR 5–12 LPA (M.Pharm/hospital); INR 15–30 LPA (R&D/regulatory)
careers.future_scope: Strong demand driven by India's pharmaceutical sector (world's largest generic drug supplier), expanding hospital pharmacies, clinical trial growth, and regulatory career opportunities with CDSCO.

careers.description: Applies engineering principles to design and develop medical equipment, prosthetics, diagnostic devices, and healthcare technologies. Works at the intersection of medicine and engineering.
education_required: B.Tech in Biomedical Engineering
B.Tech in Electronics/Electrical + specialization
M.Tech in Biomedical Engineering or Medical Devices
M.Sc in Biomedical Science (research track)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Tech Biomedical Engineering from recognized institutions. For R&D roles at medical device companies (Siemens Healthineers, GE Healthcare, Philips), M.Tech or international MS degrees are preferred.
entrance_exams: GATE Biomedical Engineering (BM paper, new) or GATE EC
GATE Life Sciences (for M.Sc research track)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM or PCB
Pursue B.Tech Biomedical Engineering
Learn signal processing, electronics, and biomedical instrumentation
Intern at hospital biomedical department or medical device company
Start as Biomedical Engineer Trainee in hospital or device firm
Pursue M.Tech Biomedical or Regulatory Affairs certification
Advance to Senior Biomedical Engineer → R&D Engineer
Specialize in medical imaging, implants, wearables, or diagnostics
Move to regulatory affairs, clinical engineering, or product management
careers.experience_level: Trainee → Biomedical Engineer → Senior Engineer → Project Lead → R&D Manager
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry); INR 8–18 LPA (5 years); INR 20–45 LPA (R&D/regulatory senior roles)
careers.future_scope: Growing rapidly due to India's medical device manufacturing push, hospital expansion, wearable health tech, AI diagnostics, and global demand for biomedical innovators.

careers.description: Studies the environment, ecosystems, and the impact of human activities. Works on pollution control, environmental impact assessment, conservation, and sustainable development policy.
education_required: B.Sc in Environmental Science, Botany, Zoology, or Chemistry
M.Sc in Environmental Science or Environmental Management
M.Tech in Environmental Engineering (alternate route)
PhD (for research or academic roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: M.Sc Environmental Science is the standard for most roles. EIA roles require certification from MoEFCC-recognized consultants. Government roles (PCBs, CPCB) often require M.Sc + competitive exams.
entrance_exams: CSIR-UGC NET (Environmental Science — for JRF and lectureship)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCB or PCM
Pursue B.Sc Environmental Science or related science
Pursue M.Sc Environmental Science
Clear CSIR-UGC NET for lectureship or research fellowship
Work as EIA Consultant, Environmental Analyst, or Lab Technician
Gain MoEFCC EIA accreditation for consulting
Advance to Senior Environmental Consultant → Environmental Manager
Pursue PhD for academic or senior policy roles
Work with government bodies: CPCB, SPCBs, MoEFCC, TERI, NGOs
tools_or_technologies: Environmental monitoring instruments (DO meter, pH meter, sound level meter)
careers.experience_level: Research Assistant → Environmental Analyst → Senior Analyst → Environmental Manager → Chief Environmental Officer
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry); INR 7–15 LPA (5 years); INR 18–35 LPA (senior/consulting); Government scale varies
careers.future_scope: Growing importance due to climate change, India's green energy transition, mandatory EIA requirements, corporate ESG mandates, and international climate finance (Green Climate Fund projects).

careers.description: Conducts original research to advance scientific knowledge in fields such as physics, chemistry, biology, or materials science. Works at universities, national labs, or R&D divisions of companies.
education_required: B.Sc in relevant science discipline
M.Sc in specialized field
PhD (essential for independent research positions)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: PhD from a reputed institution (IITs, IISc, TIFR, IISER, CSIR Labs) is the minimum for independent research scientist positions. Postdoctoral experience is often required for faculty or senior scientist roles.
certifications: CSIR-UGC NET JRF (for research fellowship funding)
Patent filing and intellectual property certification
entrance_exams: CSIR-UGC NET (for JRF fellowship and lectureship)
IISER Aptitude Test (for IISER BS-MS programs)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM or PCB
Pursue B.Sc in relevant science with first-class marks
Clear IIT JAM for M.Sc at IIT/IISc or top state universities
Clear CSIR-UGC NET or JEST/TIFR for PhD fellowship
Complete PhD (4–6 years) with published research output
Pursue postdoctoral position in India or abroad
Join as Assistant Research Scientist or Assistant Professor
Build research group, secure grants (DST, DBT, SERB)
Progress to Associate → Senior Scientist / Professor
tools_or_technologies: Bioinformatics tools (if life sciences: BLAST, Bioconductor)
careers.experience_level: PhD Student → Postdoctoral Fellow → Research Associate → Scientist B/C → Senior Scientist → Principal Scientist
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–5 LPA (PhD stipend); INR 6–10 LPA (postdoc/RA); INR 10–20 LPA (Scientist C/D); INR 20–40 LPA (Principal Scientist / faculty equivalent)
careers.future_scope: Growing investment in Indian R&D (National Research Foundation), space science (ISRO expansion), biotech, advanced materials, quantum computing, and defense R&D (DRDO).

careers.description: Collects, processes, and performs statistical analysis on large datasets to identify trends, generate reports, and support business decision-making across industries.
required_skills: SQL for data querying and manipulation
Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, Power Query)
Python or R for data analysis
education_required: Bachelor's in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Commerce, or Economics
PG Diploma or M.Sc in Data Analytics (beneficial)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: No single mandatory degree. Strong SQL, Excel, and visualization skills often matter more than degree specifics. Domain knowledge in finance, healthcare, or retail adds significant value.
certifications: Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300)
SQL for Data Science (UC Davis / Coursera)
entrance_exams: JEE / State CETs (for B.Tech/B.Sc CS route)
No mandatory entrance exam for analyst roles
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with Math/Statistics background
Pursue B.Sc Statistics/CS/Mathematics or B.Com
Learn SQL, Excel, and Python/R fundamentals
Complete data analytics certification (Google/IBM)
Build a portfolio of analytics projects
Start as Junior Data Analyst or Business Analyst Intern
Progress to Mid-Level Data Analyst with domain expertise
Pursue Power BI/Tableau certification
Advance to Senior Analyst or transition to Data Scientist track
careers.experience_level: Junior Data Analyst → Data Analyst → Senior Data Analyst → Lead Analyst → Analytics Manager
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3.5–7 LPA (entry); INR 10–20 LPA (3–5 years); INR 25–40 LPA (senior/lead analyst)
careers.future_scope: High demand across all industries. Strong stepping stone to Data Science, Business Intelligence, or Product Analytics. Increasing automation of basic tasks will shift demand toward interpretation and strategy.

careers.description: Designs, implements, and manages computer network infrastructure including LANs, WANs, data centers, cloud networks, and telecommunications systems.
required_skills: Cloud networking (AWS VPC, Azure Virtual Network)
education_required: B.Tech/B.E. in Computer Science, IT, or Electronics
B.Sc Computer Science or IT
Diploma in Networking (entry-level roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Industry certifications (CCNA, CCNP) often outweigh degree specifics for networking roles. Many professionals enter via diploma + CCNA pathway.
entrance_exams: No mandatory government exam for private sector networking roles
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM or any science stream
Pursue B.Tech CS/IT or B.Sc CS
Earn CompTIA Network+ and Cisco CCNA
Start as Network Technician or L1 Support Engineer
Gain hands-on experience with routers, switches, and firewalls
Earn CCNP Enterprise / Security certification
Advance to Network Engineer → Senior Network Engineer
Specialize in cloud networking, SDN, or cybersecurity
Pursue CCIE for architect-level roles
careers.experience_level: L1 Support → L2 Network Engineer → Senior Network Engineer → Network Architect → Head of Network Operations
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry with CCNA); INR 8–18 LPA (CCNP level); INR 20–45 LPA (architect/CCIE level)
careers.future_scope: Critical infrastructure role with growing demand in cloud networking, SD-WAN, 5G deployment, data center networking, and zero-trust network architecture.

careers.description: Provides legal advice, drafts legal documents, represents clients in courts, and ensures compliance with laws in areas such as criminal, civil, corporate, intellectual property, and constitutional law.
required_skills: Knowledge of Indian Penal Code, CPC, CrPC, Constitution
education_required: LLB (3-year, after graduation) — standard route
BA LLB / B.Com LLB / B.Sc LLB (5-year integrated) — preferred for early entry
LLM (Master of Laws) for specialization
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Bar Council of India (BCI) registration and enrollment with State Bar Council is mandatory to practice. CLAT is optional but required for NLU admissions. Some state universities have their own law entrance exams.
certifications: Mediation Certification (Supreme Court / High Court mediation centers)
Company Secretary (CS) dual qualification (for corporate law track)
entrance_exams: CLAT (Common Law Admission Test — for NLUs)
State-level law entrance exams (DU LLB, MH CET Law, AP LAWCET)
Judicial Services Examination (for becoming a civil judge)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream with min. 45% marks
Clear CLAT / AILET for NLU (5-year BA LLB) or pursue graduation then LLB
Complete BA LLB / B.Com LLB or 3-year LLB
Enroll with the State Bar Council after clearing All India Bar Exam (AIBE)
Practice under a senior advocate for 2–5 years
Specialize in Corporate, Criminal, IP, Family, or Constitutional law
Build independent practice or join a law firm
Pursue LLM for academia or senior corporate counsel roles
Appear for Judicial Services Exam for judgeship, or target High Court/Supreme Court
careers.experience_level: Junior Advocate → Advocate → Senior Advocate → Partner (Law Firm) → Judicial Officer / Senior Counsel
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2–5 LPA (junior/pupillage); INR 8–20 LPA (5 years, mid-level); INR 30–150 LPA+ (senior corporate counsel / top litigation partners)
careers.future_scope: Growing demand in corporate law (M&A, startup legal), cybercrime, IP law, arbitration, and environmental law. Judicial services offer stable government careers. Global LLM opens international law firm opportunities.

careers.career_name: Civil Servant (IAS / IPS / IFS) — UPSC CSE
careers.description: A senior government officer (IAS, IPS, IFS, etc.) responsible for public administration, policy implementation, law enforcement, or foreign relations at state and national levels.
required_skills: General awareness and current affairs (national and international)
education_required: Bachelor's degree in any discipline from a recognized university (minimum eligibility)
No specific degree required — but Arts, Humanities, Political Science, and Public Administration graduates have content advantage for optional subjects
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Any undergraduate degree is eligible. Age limit: 21–32 years (General), with relaxations for OBC, SC/ST. Graduation must be complete by the time of examination. Final year students can apply for Prelims.
certifications: LBSNAA (Lal Bahadur Shastri National Academy of Administration) — mandatory training for IAS
Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Police Academy — IPS training
entrance_exams: UPSC Civil Services Examination (CSE) — Prelims, Mains, Interview
UPSC CAPF (for Central Armed Police Forces)
State PCS (for state civil services — HAS, PCS, RAS, etc.)
career_path_steps: Complete graduation in any discipline
Begin UPSC CSE preparation 1–2 years in advance
Clear UPSC Prelims (GS Paper I + CSAT)
Clear UPSC Mains (9 papers: GS I-IV, Essay, Optional I & II, languages)
Clear Personality Test (Interview)
Join LBSNAA / respective training academy based on allotted service
Complete foundation training and field posting
Serve as Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) / ASP / attache
Progress to District Collector / SP / IFS First Secretary
Advance to Joint Secretary, Secretary, or Ambassador level
tools_or_technologies: Census and data analysis tools (for planning)
careers.experience_level: SDM/ASP (entry) → District Collector/SP → Commissioner → Secretary → Additional Secretary → Secretary to GoI
careers.average_salary_range: INR 56,100–2,50,000/month (7th Pay Commission scale, Pay Level 10–18); IAS officers receive housing, transport, and extensive allowances
careers.future_scope: Lifelong career with significant societal impact. Roles in policy formulation, international diplomacy, law enforcement, and public development. IFS cadre allows international postings. Lateral entry reforms are gradually opening select positions.

careers.description: Studies human behavior and mental processes; provides assessment, diagnosis, and therapeutic interventions for mental health conditions. Works in clinical, educational, organizational, or research settings.
required_skills: Psychological assessment and testing (WAIS, MMPI, Rorschach)
education_required: BA/B.Sc in Psychology
MA/M.Sc in Applied Psychology or Clinical Psychology (mandatory for clinical practice)
M.Phil in Clinical Psychology (RCI-recognized — required to practice as clinical psychologist)
PhD in Psychology (for academic and research roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI) registration is mandatory for clinical psychologists in India. M.Phil Clinical Psychology (2-year, approved by RCI) is required for hospital-based clinical practice. Counselors (non-clinical) typically need MA Psychology with counseling specialization.
certifications: RCI Registration (Rehabilitation Council of India — clinical psychologists)
CBT Practitioner Certification (BABCP or Indian CBT institutes)
School Counselor Certification (NCERT or state boards)
entrance_exams: CUET-PG (for MA/M.Sc Psychology in central universities)
RCI-affiliated M.Phil entrance exams (NIMHANS, RINPAS, CIP Ranchi)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream (Science/Humanities)
Pursue BA/B.Sc in Psychology
Pursue MA/M.Sc in Clinical or Applied Psychology
Clear entrance for M.Phil Clinical Psychology (RCI-recognized institution)
Complete M.Phil (2 years including supervised clinical training)
Register with RCI as Clinical Psychologist
Work in hospital, NGO, rehabilitation center, or private practice
Pursue PhD for academic or specialized research roles
Obtain CBT/DBT/other therapy certification for specialization
tools_or_technologies: Standardized psychological tests (WAIS-IV, MMPI-2, Beck Depression Inventory)
careers.experience_level: Psychology Graduate → Trainee/Intern → Junior Clinical Psychologist → Senior Psychologist → Head of Department / Private Practitioner
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry-level in hospitals/NGOs); INR 8–18 LPA (senior clinical); INR 15–40 LPA (private practice, corporate, or academic); Academic scale: as per 7th Pay Commission
careers.future_scope: Mental health awareness surge, teletherapy growth, corporate wellness programs, school mental health mandates, and increasing integration with neuroscience and AI make this a high-growth field in India.

careers.description: Researches, investigates, and reports news and information across print, digital, broadcast, and social media platforms. Holds power to influence public opinion and ensure accountability.
required_skills: Writing and editing for multiple media formats
education_required: BA in Journalism and Mass Communication
BA in English/Political Science/History (lateral entry route)
MA in Journalism / Mass Communication (preferred for senior roles)
PG Diploma in Journalism
careers.specific_degree_requirements: No single mandatory degree. Portfolio of published work often matters more. IIMC, AJK-MCRC (JMI), ACJ (Chennai) PG diploma programs are highly respected in India.
certifications: Google News Initiative Digital Journalism Certificate
Verified Press ID (PIB accreditation for central government)
entrance_exams: CUET-PG (for MA Journalism in central universities)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream
Pursue BA Journalism and Mass Communication or BA English/Humanities
Build portfolio through college newspaper, blogging, or freelancing
Pursue MA Journalism or IIMC PG Diploma
Start as Reporter / Sub-Editor / Content Writer at media house
Cover a specific beat (politics, business, crime, sports, science)
Build byline reputation and network
Advance to Senior Reporter → Correspondent → Bureau Chief → Editor
Explore digital journalism, documentary filmmaking, or data journalism
tools_or_technologies: OSINT tools for investigation (Maltego, Wayback Machine)
careers.experience_level: Reporter → Senior Reporter → Correspondent → Assistant Editor → Deputy Editor → Editor-in-Chief
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2.5–5 LPA (entry); INR 7–15 LPA (5 years); INR 20–50 LPA (senior editor/anchor at major outlets); Freelance varies widely
careers.future_scope: Digital media growth, independent journalism, video journalism, and fact-checking organizations are growing. Data journalism, science journalism, and investigative journalism are premium skill areas.

careers.description: Imparts knowledge of physics concepts, principles, and practical applications to students at secondary and higher secondary levels in schools.
required_skills: In-depth subject knowledge of Physics (mechanics, electromagnetism, optics, modern physics)
education_required: B.Sc Physics (Hons) or B.Sc with Physics as main subject
B.Ed (Bachelor of Education) — mandatory for government and CBSE-affiliated private schools
M.Sc Physics (for teaching at Class 11–12 or for higher salary bands)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Ed from an NCTE-recognized institution is mandatory for government school teaching. For KV (Kendriya Vidyalaya) and NVS schools, PGT Physics requires M.Sc Physics + B.Ed. Some international or private schools may waive B.Ed if the candidate has M.Sc Physics.
certifications: CTET (Central Teacher Eligibility Test) — mandatory for central government school teaching
State TET (REET, UPTET, TNTET etc.) — for state government schools
National Initiative for School Heads and Teachers Holistic Advancement (NISHTHA) training
entrance_exams: CTET (Paper II for Class 6–8; PGT requires state exams)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM
Pursue B.Sc Physics (Hons) or B.Sc with Physics
Pursue B.Ed from NCTE-recognized institution
Clear CTET / State TET
Apply for TGT/PGT Physics positions in government/private schools
Optionally pursue M.Sc Physics for PGT-level eligibility and higher pay
Advance to Senior Teacher → Head of Department → Vice Principal → Principal
tools_or_technologies: YouTube physics channels for supplementary teaching
careers.experience_level: Junior Teacher → TGT → PGT → Senior Teacher → Head of Department → Vice Principal
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2.5–4 LPA (private unaided schools); INR 5–9 LPA (KV/NV schools as per 7th Pay Commission); INR 6–12 LPA (senior international schools)
careers.future_scope: Strong job security in government schools. Growing demand for quality science teachers in private, international, and online education platforms. Coaching institutes offer high income for experienced Physics teachers.

careers.description: Teaches computer science concepts including programming, algorithms, databases, networking, and computational thinking to school students at primary, secondary, or senior secondary level.
required_skills: Programming languages relevant to curriculum (Python, Java, C++)
education_required: B.Tech/B.E. in Computer Science or IT (B.Ed may or may not be required — institution dependent)
BCA + B.Ed (for non-engineering graduates)
MCA or M.Sc CS (for PGT-level posts)
B.Ed from NCTE-recognized institution (required for government schools)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: For government schools (KV, NV, State): B.Tech CS + B.Ed OR MCA/M.Sc CS + B.Ed. Some private international/CBSE schools hire B.Tech CS holders without B.Ed if they demonstrate strong teaching aptitude. Unlike Physics or History, CS teachers in some private institutions may not require B.Ed due to talent scarcity.
certifications: CTET Paper II (for middle school CS teacher)
Python Institute PCEP (entry-level Python certification — useful for credibility)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM
Pursue B.Tech CS/IT or BCA
Pursue B.Ed (NCTE-recognized) if targeting government school teaching
Clear CTET and state TET
Apply for CS Teacher / PGT CS positions
Continuously update skills with evolving CBSE CS curriculum (Python, AI, Data Science elective)
Advance to HOD → Vice Principal → Principal
careers.experience_level: Junior CS Teacher → Senior CS Teacher → PGT CS → HOD → Vice Principal
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–5 LPA (private unaided); INR 5–10 LPA (KV/NV/reputed private schools); INR 8–15 LPA (international or high-demand schools)
careers.future_scope: Rapidly growing demand as schools integrate AI, coding, data science, and cybersecurity in curriculum. Hybrid teaching, EdTech platforms, and coaching institutes offer significant alternate income.

careers.description: Teaches history, social science, and civics to students at secondary and higher secondary levels, covering Indian and world history, political science, geography, and economics.
required_skills: Comprehensive knowledge of Indian and world history
education_required: BA History (Hons) or BA with History
B.Ed — mandatory for all government schools and most private CBSE/ICSE schools
MA History (required for PGT Social Science or PGT History positions)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: MA History + B.Ed is the standard requirement for PGT Social Science in KVS and NVS. State government schools require TET qualification. B.Ed from NCTE-recognized institution is non-negotiable for government school roles.
certifications: UGC-NET History (for higher education / lectureship track)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in Humanities stream
Pursue BA History (Hons)
Pursue MA History
Pursue B.Ed from NCTE-recognized college
Clear CTET and State TET
Apply for TGT Social Science or PGT History roles
Continue professional development through NISHTHA and DIKSHA
Advance to HOD → Vice Principal → Principal
careers.experience_level: Junior Teacher (TGT) → Senior Teacher → PGT → HOD Social Science → Vice Principal
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2–4 LPA (private unaided); INR 4–8 LPA (KV/NV government scale); INR 5–9 LPA (reputed private schools)
careers.future_scope: Steady demand in government schools with job security. Growing interest in UPSC coaching creates parallel market for history teachers in coaching institutes at premium pay.

careers.description: Teaches, conducts research, and contributes to knowledge in a specific discipline at degree college or university level. Engages in academic publishing, student mentoring, and institutional governance.
education_required: Bachelor's in relevant discipline
Master's in the same or related field
PhD (mandatory for Assistant Professor roles in universities)
Postdoctoral experience (preferred for research-intensive institutions)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: UGC-NET with JRF or PhD is mandatory for Assistant Professor roles in central/state universities under UGC guidelines. PhD from NAAC-accredited or IIT/IISc-equivalent institutions strongly preferred for top colleges.
certifications: UGC-NET JRF (Junior Research Fellowship — for PhD funding and lectureship)
SLET/SET (State-level Eligibility Test — alternative to NET for state colleges)
entrance_exams: UGC-NET (National Eligibility Test) — for Assistant Professor and JRF
GATE + PhD (alternate route for science/engineering professors)
career_path_steps: Complete relevant Bachelor's degree
Pursue Master's degree in specialization
Clear UGC-NET/CSIR-NET with JRF for PhD fellowship
Complete PhD (4–6 years) with published research
Pursue postdoctoral position or Guest Lecturer role
Apply for Assistant Professor position
Publish research papers and secure research grants (SERB, DST, UGC)
Progress to Associate Professor (after 8 years + research output)
Advance to Professor (after 10+ years + significant contributions)
careers.experience_level: PhD Candidate → Postdoc / Guest Lecturer → Assistant Professor → Associate Professor → Professor → Dean
careers.average_salary_range: INR 5–10 LPA (Assistant Professor, 7th CPC Level 10); INR 12–18 LPA (Associate Professor); INR 18–28 LPA (Professor); IIT/IIM scales are significantly higher
careers.future_scope: National Education Policy 2020 is transforming higher education, creating more research positions and interdisciplinary roles. Growing demand for professors in private universities, online education, and international collaborations.

careers.description: Works to improve the welfare of individuals, families, and communities facing social, economic, or psychological challenges through counseling, advocacy, rehabilitation, and community development.
required_skills: Knowledge of social welfare schemes (government programs)
education_required: BA in Social Work
BSW (Bachelor of Social Work)
MSW (Master of Social Work) — preferred for senior roles
PhD in Social Work (for academic and senior policy roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: MSW from a recognized institution is the standard for professional social work positions. TISS (Mumbai), Delhi University, JNU, and Jamia are top institutions. Some NGOs hire BA/BSW graduates for field roles.
certifications: Certificate in Child Rights and Protection (UNICEF / TISS)
entrance_exams: TISS NET (for MSW at TISS campuses — nationally competitive)
University-specific MSW entrance tests (DU, BHU, JNU, Jamia)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream
Pursue BA/BSW in Social Work
Pursue MSW with specialization (Medical, Psychiatric, Rural, Urban, HR, or Community Development)
Complete field placement / social work practicum during MSW
Join as Social Work Trainee, Field Officer, or Case Manager at NGO/hospital
Build expertise in a domain: child protection, disability, mental health, women empowerment
Progress to Program Manager → Senior Social Worker
Pursue UGC-NET for academic roles or PhD for research/policy
Move toward NGO Director, policy advisor, or UN/INGO roles
careers.experience_level: Field Worker → Social Work Assistant → Social Worker → Senior Social Worker → Program Manager → NGO Director
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2–4 LPA (NGO entry); INR 5–10 LPA (Program Manager); INR 12–25 LPA (Director/International NGO); Government social worker scales vary by department
careers.future_scope: Growing need in mental health outreach, disaster response, urban migration management, women's safety, tribal welfare, and UN SDG implementation across India.

careers.description: A globally recognized finance professional specializing in investment analysis, portfolio management, equity research, risk assessment, and asset management.
education_required: Bachelor's degree in any discipline (or enrolled in final year)
4,000 hours of relevant professional experience (for charter award)
CFA Program completion (Level I, II, III)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: The CFA charter is awarded by CFA Institute (USA) and requires passing all three exam levels plus 4,000 hours of qualifying work experience. The charter itself is the key qualification — no specific degree is mandatory.
certifications: CFA Charter (CFA Institute) — Level I, II, III
career_path_steps: Complete graduation in any discipline
Register for CFA Level I (computer-based testing, multiple windows annually)
Clear CFA Level I (covering 10 topic areas)
Clear CFA Level II (application and analysis of investment tools)
Clear CFA Level III (portfolio management and wealth planning)
Accumulate 4,000 hours of qualifying investment experience
Receive CFA charter
Work in equity research, asset management, risk, or investment banking
Progress to Portfolio Manager, Research Director, or CIO
careers.experience_level: Research Analyst → Senior Analyst → Portfolio Manager → Director of Research → CIO
careers.average_salary_range: INR 8–15 LPA (entry with Level I/II); INR 20–45 LPA (charter holder, 3–7 years); INR 60–150 LPA+ (senior PM/CIO level)
careers.future_scope: Gold standard in investment management globally. Growing demand from Indian mutual funds, pension funds, family offices, and hedge funds. High international mobility.

careers.description: Uses mathematics, statistics, and financial theory to assess and manage financial risk in insurance, pension, investment, and health sectors.
required_skills: Financial mathematics and time value of money
Communication of complex risk to management
education_required: B.Sc or B.Tech in Mathematics, Statistics, or Actuarial Science
Actuarial Science B.Sc (specialized programs at Amity, Christ University, etc.)
Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (IFoA) or IAI exam pathway
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Actuarial career is exam-driven. Institute of Actuaries of India (IAI) offers Fellowship program. Alternatively, pursuing exams through Society of Actuaries (SOA, USA) or IFoA (UK) is common. Strong mathematical foundation is essential.
certifications: Fellow of Society of Actuaries (FSA) — USA
entrance_exams: IAI Actuarial Common Entrance Test (ACET) — for student membership
SOA Exam P, FM, IFM, etc. (for US pathway)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with strong math background
Pursue B.Sc Mathematics/Statistics or Actuarial Science
Register with IAI and clear ACET
Clear Core Technical (CT1–CT9 / CS/CM series) exams progressively
Join an insurance or consulting firm as Actuarial Analyst
Clear Core Applications (CA) and Subject (SA) papers
Become Associate of IAI (AIAI) after Core stage
Clear remaining Fellowship exams and become FIAI
Advance to Chief Actuary or head of risk at insurer
careers.experience_level: Actuarial Analyst → Actuarial Associate → Senior Actuary → Appointed Actuary → Chief Actuary
careers.average_salary_range: INR 5–10 LPA (entry); INR 15–30 LPA (associate/5+ CT papers cleared); INR 40–100 LPA (Fellow Actuary/Chief Actuary)
careers.future_scope: Severe shortage of qualified actuaries in India. Massive demand from IRDAI-regulated insurance sector, NPS/pension funds, government health insurance (Ayushman Bharat), and ERM in banks.

careers.description: Develops plans and programs for the use of land and the growth of communities. Works on urban development, housing, transport, infrastructure, and sustainable city planning.
required_skills: Urban design and land use planning
education_required: B.Tech in Civil Engineering / Architecture (entry route for PG planning)
B.Arch (5-year architecture degree)
M.Plan / M.Tech Urban Planning (2-year postgraduate — primary qualification)
PhD in Urban Planning / Regional Planning (for research/academic roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: M.Plan from SPA (School of Planning and Architecture, New Delhi/Bhopal/Vijayawada) or CEPT University is the gold standard. Entry to M.Plan requires GATE AR or specific state-level planning entrance exams.
certifications: AICP — American Institute of Certified Planners (for international practice)
entrance_exams: JEE/State CET (for B.Tech Civil, entry to planning PG route)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM or arts
Pursue B.Tech Civil or B.Arch
Clear GATE AR for M.Plan at SPA/CEPT/IIT Kharagpur
Complete M.Plan with specialization (housing, transport, urban design, environment)
Join urban local body, DUDA, CMDA, or private consultancy
Work on Smart Cities Mission, AMRUT, RERA compliance projects
Advance to Senior Planner → Chief Planner → Director Town Planning
careers.experience_level: Junior Planner → Urban Planner → Senior Planner → Chief Planner → Director
careers.average_salary_range: INR 4–8 LPA (entry); INR 10–20 LPA (5 years); INR 20–45 LPA (senior/government director level)
careers.future_scope: Central to India's Smart Cities Mission, AMRUT 2.0, Metro Rail projects, transit-oriented development, and climate-resilient urban design. Growing international opportunities with climate-focused urban planning.

careers.description: Defines product vision, roadmap, and strategy. Works with engineering, design, marketing, and sales teams to build and launch successful digital products that meet user and business needs.
required_skills: User story writing and requirements gathering
education_required: B.Tech/B.E. in Computer Science or related field (most common)
MBA in Technology Management or General Management (common for career switchers)
BBA/B.Sc with strong tech and analytics background (alternate route)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: No mandatory degree. B.Tech CS + 2–3 years of engineering experience or MBA from top institutes (IIMs, ISB) are the most common entry paths. Strong portfolios and side projects can substitute for traditional credentials at many startups.
career_path_steps: Complete B.Tech CS or related field OR pursue MBA
Gain 2–3 years of experience as Software Engineer, Data Analyst, or Business Analyst
Transition to Associate Product Manager (APM) or junior PM
Lead end-to-end product features with cross-functional teams
Build product intuition through user research and data analysis
Advance to Product Manager → Senior PM → Group PM
Specialize: B2B SaaS, consumer apps, platform, or AI products
Progress to Director of Product → VP Product → CPO
careers.experience_level: APM / Junior PM → Product Manager → Senior PM → Group PM → Director → VP Product → CPO
careers.average_salary_range: INR 8–15 LPA (APM/junior); INR 20–40 LPA (PM, 3–5 years); INR 50–100 LPA+ (Director/VP at top tech firms)
careers.future_scope: One of the most sought-after roles in tech. Demand driven by India's startup ecosystem, SaaS companies, and global product teams. AI product management is emerging as a premium subspecialty.

careers.description: Bridges development and operations by automating software delivery pipelines, managing infrastructure as code, and ensuring reliability, scalability, and security of production systems.
required_skills: CI/CD pipeline design (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)
education_required: B.Tech/B.E. in Computer Science or IT
B.Sc Computer Science (alternate route)
Diploma in Cloud Computing (for bootcamp transitions)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Tech CS/IT is the standard. DevOps is often entered via software engineering or system administration roles. Cloud certifications hold significant weight alongside or above degree qualifications.
entrance_exams: No mandatory exam for industry entry — certifications more relevant
career_path_steps: Complete B.Tech CS/IT
Start as Software Engineer or Linux System Administrator
Learn Docker and Kubernetes hands-on
Set up and manage CI/CD pipelines (Jenkins, GitHub Actions)
Earn AWS Associate Certification
Transition to DevOps Engineer or SRE role
Earn CKA and Terraform certifications
Advance to Senior DevOps → DevOps Lead → Platform Engineering Lead
Specialize in SRE, MLOps, DataOps, or Cloud Architecture
careers.experience_level: Junior DevOps → DevOps Engineer → Senior DevOps → DevOps Lead → Platform Engineering Manager → VP Infrastructure
careers.average_salary_range: INR 6–12 LPA (entry); INR 18–35 LPA (3–5 years); INR 40–80 LPA (senior/lead/architect)
careers.future_scope: High demand as every company moves to cloud-native development. Site Reliability Engineering (SRE), MLOps, FinOps, and Platform Engineering are high-paying evolutions of the DevOps role.

careers.description: Designs intuitive, accessible, and visually appealing digital product interfaces by conducting user research, creating wireframes, prototypes, and collaborating with engineering teams.
education_required: B.Des in Interaction Design, Visual Communication, or Product Design
B.Tech CS + UX self-study / certification (common career switch route)
BA in Design or Fine Arts
M.Des in Interaction Design (for senior/academic roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: NID (National Institute of Design) and IIT (Industrial Design Centre) B.Des/M.Des programs are premium. However, portfolios carry equal or greater weight than degrees in UX hiring. Career transitions from CS/engineering backgrounds are common.
entrance_exams: UCEED (Undergraduate Common Entrance Examination for Design — for IIT IDC and NID)
CEED (Common Entrance Examination for Design — for M.Des at IITs)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream
Pursue B.Des in Interaction/Visual Design from NID/IIT/NIFT or complete B.Tech with UX self-study
Build portfolio with case studies on real or practice projects
Complete Google UX Design Certificate or similar
Internship as UX Intern or UI Designer
Join as Junior UX/UI Designer
Progress to UX Designer → Senior Designer → Lead Designer
Specialize in product design, motion design, or design ops
Advance to Design Lead → Head of Design → VP Design / CDO
careers.experience_level: Intern → Junior Designer → UX/UI Designer → Senior Designer → Lead Designer → Design Director → CDO
careers.average_salary_range: INR 4–8 LPA (entry); INR 12–25 LPA (3–5 years); INR 30–60 LPA (senior lead/director at top product companies)
careers.future_scope: Growing demand for design in SaaS, fintech, healthtech, and AI products. Voice UI, AR/VR/XR design, AI-augmented design tools, and design systems engineering are emerging high-value subspecialties.

careers.description: Studies production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services. Analyzes economic data, creates models, advises on policy, and works in research, government, or corporate settings.
required_skills: Report writing and policy brief preparation
education_required: B.Sc/BA in Economics (Hons)
MA/M.Sc in Economics (required for professional roles)
PhD in Economics (for research economist or faculty positions)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: MA Economics from DSE (Delhi School of Economics), JNU, IGIDR, or ISI is the gold standard in India. For corporate or government roles, MA Economics suffices. PhD is needed for research institutions (RBI, NITI Aayog, world bank), universities, or international organizations.
certifications: World Bank MOOC on Economic Development
entrance_exams: Delhi School of Economics Entrance Test
ISI Admission Test (for M.Stat and related programs)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with strong Math and Economics background
Pursue BA/B.Sc Economics (Hons) from top college
Clear DSE/JNU/IGIDR entrance for MA Economics
Complete MA with focus on econometrics and research methodology
Join as Research Associate at think tank, RBI, or consulting firm
Pursue PhD from top Indian or international institution
Work as Policy Economist, Research Economist, or Consultant
Advance to Senior Economist → Lead Economist → Chief Economist
tools_or_technologies: FRED / World Bank / IMF data portals
careers.experience_level: Research Assistant → Research Associate → Economist → Senior Economist → Lead Economist → Chief Economist
careers.average_salary_range: INR 4–8 LPA (research associate); INR 10–20 LPA (economist, 5 years); INR 25–60 LPA (senior/chief economist at RBI, MNC, international org)
careers.future_scope: High demand in policy think tanks (NITI Aayog, RBI, IMF, World Bank), fintech data analytics, corporate strategy, and global development organizations. Behavioral economics is a growing field.

careers.description: Diagnoses and treats dental conditions including cavities, gum disease, orthodontic issues, and oral surgery. Specializes in restorative, cosmetic, or surgical dentistry.
education_required: 12th with PCB
BDS — Bachelor of Dental Surgery (4 years + 1 year internship)
MDS — Master of Dental Surgery (3 years, specialization) — optional
careers.specific_degree_requirements: NEET-UG is mandatory for BDS admission. Dental Council of India (DCI) registration is compulsory to practice. MDS through NEET-MDS for specialization.
certifications: Fellowship in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery (FOMS)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCB (min 50% for NEET eligibility)
Clear NEET-UG and secure BDS admission
Complete BDS (4 years + 1 year compulsory internship)
Register with State Dental Council and DCI
Begin practice as General Dental Surgeon or join hospital
Prepare for NEET-MDS and pursue specialization (Orthodontics, Endodontics, Prosthodontics, Oral Surgery)
Complete MDS (3 years)
Open private dental clinic or join dental chain/hospital
Pursue advanced implantology or cosmetic dentistry courses
tools_or_technologies: Dental practice management software (Dentbird, Curve Dental)
careers.experience_level: BDS Intern → Junior Dentist → Associate Dentist → Specialist (MDS) → Senior Consultant → Clinic Owner
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (fresh BDS); INR 8–20 LPA (experienced/private practice); INR 15–50 LPA (MDS specialist/cosmetic dentist); Own clinic income varies significantly
careers.future_scope: Growing oral healthcare awareness, dental tourism, dental chains expansion (Clove Dental, Sabka Dentist), and cosmetic dentistry demand are strong growth drivers. Tele-dentistry is an emerging area.

careers.description: Assesses dietary needs and provides evidence-based nutrition advice and therapeutic diet plans for individuals managing weight, chronic diseases, sports performance, or general wellness.
required_skills: Clinical nutrition and medical nutrition therapy
Nutrient calculation and meal planning software
education_required: B.Sc in Nutrition and Dietetics / Home Science (Food & Nutrition)
M.Sc Dietetics/Clinical Nutrition (required for hospital clinical roles)
PG Diploma in Dietetics
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Indian Dietetic Association (IDA) membership requires M.Sc or PG Diploma in Dietetics with supervised clinical training. Registered Dietitian (RD) status requires IDA/RCI-recognized qualifications for clinical settings.
entrance_exams: State-level science entrance exams for B.Sc admission
IIT JAM or university entrance for M.Sc
No central mandatory exam for practice — IDA membership is the key credential
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCB
Pursue B.Sc Nutrition and Dietetics
Pursue M.Sc Clinical Nutrition or PG Diploma in Dietetics
Complete supervised clinical internship (minimum 6 months)
Register with IDA as Registered Dietitian
Work in hospital, wellness center, or sports organization
Build online practice / telehealth nutrition consulting
Specialize: Oncology nutrition, pediatric nutrition, sports nutrition, bariatric
Advance to Head Dietitian / HOD / independent practice
careers.experience_level: Intern Dietitian → Junior Dietitian → Dietitian → Senior Dietitian → Chief Dietitian / Consultant
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–5 LPA (entry hospital); INR 6–12 LPA (5 years); INR 15–30 LPA (consultant/online practice with brand); Sports dietitian for athletes can earn significantly more
careers.future_scope: Booming wellness industry, diabetes epidemic, sports nutrition, and corporate wellness programs are driving growth. Online nutrition consulting and social media-based practice are high-income alternate models.

careers.description: Applies scientific methods to analyze physical evidence from crime scenes to assist in criminal investigations and court proceedings. Specializes in areas like DNA analysis, toxicology, ballistics, or digital forensics.
education_required: B.Sc in Forensic Science, Chemistry, Biology, or Physics
M.Sc in Forensic Science (preferred for FSL/CBI/police roles)
LLB (additional, for forensic legal roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: M.Sc Forensic Science from GFSU (Gujarat Forensic Sciences University), Panjab University, or Osmania University is highly valued for government forensic labs. CBI, CID, and state FSLs recruit through government exams.
entrance_exams: IIT JAM (for M.Sc in science, then specialize)
SSC (for some forensic assistant roles in government labs)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCB or PCM
Pursue B.Sc Forensic Science or B.Sc Chemistry/Biology
Pursue M.Sc Forensic Science (GFSU, Panjab University preferred)
Intern at state Forensic Science Laboratory (FSL)
Join as Forensic Analyst or Scientific Assistant at FSL/CBI
Specialize: DNA, toxicology, digital forensics, or document examination
Advance to Senior Scientist → Expert Witness → Director FSL
careers.experience_level: Scientific Assistant → Junior Scientific Officer → Scientific Officer → Senior Scientist → Director FSL
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (FSL entry); INR 7–14 LPA (senior officer); INR 15–25 LPA (Director/private forensic consulting)
careers.future_scope: Growing demand with increasing cyber crimes, NDPS cases, digital forensics needs, and court-mandated scientific analysis. Private forensic consulting for corporates and law firms is an emerging area.

careers.description: Provides emotional support, career guidance, and therapeutic counseling to individuals facing personal, academic, or career-related challenges. Works in schools, colleges, NGOs, or private practice.
required_skills: Career assessment tools (MBTI, Holland Code, aptitude tests)
Knowledge of mental health and psychoeducation
education_required: BA/B.Sc in Psychology
MA in Applied Psychology with Counseling specialization
PG Diploma in Counseling and Psychotherapy
M.Ed with guidance and counseling specialization (for school setting)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: For school guidance counselors, M.Ed with counseling specialization is recognized. For clinical counseling, MA Psychology with counseling + therapy certifications. RCI M.Phil is required for clinical psychologist title specifically.
certifications: IAGC (Indian Association of Guidance Counselors) membership
Career Counselor Certification — iCARE, Univariety, CareerPrinceton
entrance_exams: CTET / State TET (for school counselor in government schools)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream
Pursue BA/B.Sc Psychology
Pursue MA Applied Psychology with counseling focus
Complete internship at school, NGO, or hospital counseling center
Earn CBT or career counseling certification
Work as Junior Counselor at school or NGO
Build specialization: school counseling, career, or wellness
Progress to Senior Counselor → Head of Student Wellness → Independent Practice
tools_or_technologies: Mindfulness apps (Calm, Headspace for supplementary tools)
careers.experience_level: Trainee Counselor → Junior Counselor → Counselor → Senior Counselor → Head of Wellness / Independent Practitioner
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2.5–5 LPA (school/NGO entry); INR 5–12 LPA (senior); INR 15–30 LPA (corporate wellness/independent practice)
careers.future_scope: National Mental Health Policy mandates school counselors in all schools. Corporate mental health programs, employee assistance programs (EAPs), and edtech wellness integrations are growing rapidly.

careers.description: Designs buildings and structures, balancing aesthetics, functionality, safety, and sustainability. Oversees construction from concept to completion for residential, commercial, institutional, and urban projects.
education_required: B.Arch (Bachelor of Architecture — 5 years)
M.Arch (Master of Architecture — 2 years, for specialization)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Arch must be from a Council of Architecture (CoA)-approved institution. CoA registration is mandatory to practice as an Architect in India (under the Architects Act, 1972). JEE Paper 2 / NATA is required for B.Arch admission.
certifications: GRIHA Evaluator (Green Rating for Integrated Habitat Assessment)
entrance_exams: NATA (National Aptitude Test in Architecture) — for most B.Arch colleges
JEE Main Paper 2 (for NITs/SPAs/IITs offering B.Arch)
GATE AR (for M.Arch at IITs and SPAs)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with Math (mandatory for architecture)
Clear NATA / JEE Paper 2
Complete B.Arch (5 years)
Register with the Council of Architecture (CoA)
Work in an architecture firm as Junior Architect
Build design portfolio across residential, commercial, and institutional projects
Pursue M.Arch for academic or specialized sustainable/urban architecture
Become a licensed independent architect and open practice
Advance to Principal Architect → Design Director
careers.experience_level: Junior Architect → Architect → Senior Architect → Associate → Principal Architect → Design Director
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry); INR 8–18 LPA (5 years); INR 20–50 LPA (senior/principal); Own practice income is highly variable
careers.future_scope: India's real estate growth, Smart Cities Mission, affordable housing projects, green building mandates, and heritage conservation create sustained demand. Sustainable and parametric architecture are high-value niches.

careers.description: Operates commercial aircraft transporting passengers or cargo. Responsible for pre-flight checks, navigation, communication with ATC, and safe operation of the aircraft.
education_required: 12th with Physics and Mathematics (mandatory)
CPL (Commercial Pilot License) — from DGCA-approved flying school
Type Rating on specific aircraft (B737, A320, etc.) — required for airline employment
careers.specific_degree_requirements: DGCA (Directorate General of Civil Aviation) issues CPL after medical clearance (Class I), minimum 200 flight hours, and passing 8 DGCA written exams plus skill tests. Age minimum is 17 (student pilot) and 18 (CPL).
certifications: Type Rating (B737, A320, ATR72 — mandatory per aircraft type)
ATPL (Airline Transport Pilot License — for Captain upgrade)
entrance_exams: DGCA CPL Written Exams (Air Navigation, Meteorology, Air Regulations, Technical General, Technical Specific)
Airline aptitude and assessment tests (varied by airline)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with Physics and Mathematics
Pass DGCA Class I Medical Exam
Enroll in DGCA-approved flying training organization (FTO)
Complete Private Pilot License (PPL) — 40 hours minimum
Accumulate 200 flight hours (total)
Clear all 8 DGCA CPL theory papers
Obtain Instrument Rating and Multi-Engine Rating
Apply for CPL from DGCA
Complete airline selection process and Type Rating training
Join as First Officer → upgrade to Captain after 1,500 ATPL hours
tools_or_technologies: Glass cockpit avionics (Garmin G1000, Honeywell Primus)
Flight simulators (Full Flight Simulator — Level D)
careers.experience_level: Student Pilot → PPL → CPL → First Officer → Senior First Officer → Captain → Training Captain
careers.average_salary_range: CPL training cost: INR 40–80 lakhs; First Officer: INR 15–25 LPA; Captain: INR 40–120 LPA; Narrow body Captain at major carrier: INR 80–1.5 Cr PA
careers.future_scope: India's aviation sector is one of the fastest growing globally. IndiGo, Air India, and new carriers are creating thousands of pilot positions. Pilot shortage is a global structural issue for the next decade.

careers.description: Designs, operates, and maintains propulsion systems, machinery, and onboard systems of ships, submarines, and offshore platforms. Works aboard vessels or in shipyards and consulting.
required_skills: Marine propulsion systems (diesel engines, gas turbines)
Electrical and automation systems on ships
education_required: B.E./B.Tech Marine Engineering (4 years) from IMU-approved institution
B.Sc Nautical Science (for Deck Officers — different track)
Diploma in Marine Engineering (for junior marine engineer track)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Indian Maritime University (IMU) or DG Shipping-approved college. STCW certification (Standards of Training, Certification and Watchkeeping) is internationally mandatory. Pre-sea training and cadetship are compulsory.
entrance_exams: IMU-CET (Indian Maritime University Common Entrance Test)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM (Math and Physics essential)
Clear IMU-CET and join approved Marine Engineering college
Complete pre-sea training and cadetship
Sail as Marine Engineer Cadet
Clear MEO Class 4 CoC (Certificate of Competency)
Progress through Class 4 → Class 2 → Class 1 Engineer certificates
Achieve Chief Engineer rank (Class I CoC required)
Transition to shore-based roles: Superintendent, DPA, or Shipyard
tools_or_technologies: Ship maintenance management systems (AMOS, DNV GL Smart)
Vibration analysis and condition monitoring tools
Electronic Chart Display and Information System (ECDIS)
careers.experience_level: Cadet → 4th Engineer → 3rd Engineer → 2nd Engineer → Chief Engineer → Superintendent (Shore)
careers.average_salary_range: Junior Engineer (sea): USD 1,500–3,000/month; 2nd Engineer: USD 4,000–6,000/month; Chief Engineer: USD 8,000–15,000+/month
careers.future_scope: India is among the top suppliers of maritime officers globally. Growing demand for LNG, offshore, and specialized vessel engineers. Shore-based roles in shipyards, classification societies, and shipbuilding are expanding.

careers.description: Studies the Earth's structure, composition, and processes. Works in mineral exploration, oil and gas, groundwater, environmental geology, and hazard assessment.
required_skills: Remote sensing and GIS for geological applications
education_required: B.Sc Geology (Hons)
M.Sc Geology or Applied Geology (required for professional roles)
PhD Geology (for academic or senior research roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: M.Sc Geology is the minimum for GSI, ONGC, CGWB, and mining company roles. IIT JAM Geology paper is the main entry for M.Sc at IITs. UPSC exams include Geology as an optional subject for state geological survey roles.
certifications: GATE Geology and Geophysics (GG) — for PSU roles
Remote Sensing and GIS Training (IIRS, Dehradun)
entrance_exams: GSI Geological Survey of India Recruitment Exam
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCM or PCB
Pursue B.Sc Geology (Hons)
Clear IIT JAM for M.Sc Geology at IITs/IISc
Pursue M.Sc with specialization in petroleum, mining, or hydrogeology
Clear GATE GG for PSU/government geology roles
Join GSI, ONGC, CGWB, or private mining firm
Pursue PhD for academic or senior research scientist track
Advance to Senior Geologist → Deputy Director → Director
careers.experience_level: Field Geologist → Geologist → Senior Geologist → Principal Geologist → Geological Officer → Director
careers.average_salary_range: INR 4–8 LPA (entry GSI/private); INR 10–20 LPA (ONGC scale 5+ years); INR 20–45 LPA (senior/ONGC senior grade); International upstream oil companies offer higher packages
careers.future_scope: Critical for India's mineral exploration (critical minerals for EVs), oil and gas, groundwater management, disaster risk reduction (earthquake, landslide), and environmental geotechnics.

careers.description: Manages costs in construction and infrastructure projects — from initial estimates to final accounts. Ensures financial efficiency, contract compliance, and value for money in built environment projects.
required_skills: Quantity estimation and bill of quantities (BOQ)
