AutoCAD and BIM for quantity take-offs
education_required: B.Tech Civil Engineering or B.Arch
B.Sc Quantity Surveying (offered in some institutions)
M.Tech Construction Management or MSc Quantity Surveying
RICS accredited programs (for MRICS designation)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: RICS (Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors) Assessment of Professional Competence (APC) is the globally recognized professional qualification. In India, B.Tech Civil + work experience + RICS APC is the standard path.
certifications: MRICS (Member of RICS) — via APC route
Autodesk Revit / Navisworks (for BIM-based quantity surveying)
entrance_exams: RICS APC (professional competency assessment — not a written exam)
career_path_steps: Complete B.Tech Civil Engineering
Join a construction company or PMC as Graduate Trainee
Learn BOQ preparation, cost estimation, and contract administration
Pursue RICS APC candidacy through recognized employer or self-sponsorship
Complete RICS APC assessment (typically 2 years structured training)
Obtain MRICS designation
Progress to Senior QS → Associate Director → Director
Specialize in commercial management, claims, or infrastructure
careers.experience_level: Graduate QS → Quantity Surveyor → Senior QS → Associate Director → Director → Partner
careers.average_salary_range: INR 4–8 LPA (entry); INR 12–25 LPA (MRICS, 5 years); INR 30–70 LPA (Director level, international projects)
careers.future_scope: Strong growth in infrastructure projects, PPP contracts, real estate development, and international construction markets (Middle East, Africa, SE Asia). BIM-integrated cost management is a high-demand specialization.

careers.description: Plans and executes marketing campaigns across digital channels including SEO, SEM, social media, email, and content marketing to drive brand awareness, lead generation, and customer acquisition.
required_skills: Google Ads / Meta Ads campaign management
Marketing funnel design and conversion rate optimization
education_required: BBA / B.Com / BA Mass Communication (most common entry routes)
MBA in Marketing (for senior leadership track)
B.Tech (for growth marketing / data-driven roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: No mandatory degree for entry-level roles — skills and portfolio of campaigns are more important. MBA Marketing from a top B-school accelerates growth to senior brand and growth leadership positions.
certifications: Google Ads Search / Display / Video Certification
entrance_exams: CAT / MAT / XAT (for MBA Marketing route)
No mandatory exam for direct industry entry
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream
Pursue BBA / B.Com / BA Mass Communication
Learn SEO, Google Ads, and social media fundamentals through online courses
Complete Google Analytics and Google Ads certifications
Build portfolio: run live campaigns, manage social accounts, or work on freelance projects
Start as Digital Marketing Executive or Intern
Progress to Digital Marketing Analyst → Manager → Head of Digital
Specialize: SEO, PPC, influencer marketing, growth hacking, or MarTech
careers.experience_level: Digital Marketing Executive → Analyst → Manager → Senior Manager → VP Marketing / CMO
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry); INR 8–18 LPA (manager, 3–5 years); INR 25–60 LPA (VP/CMO at scaling brands); Freelance rates vary widely
careers.future_scope: Growing exponentially with D2C brand boom, OTT advertising, influencer marketing, and AI-driven personalization. Marketing technology (MarTech) and performance marketing specialists are among the most sought-after profiles.

careers.description: Plans, implements, and controls the efficient flow of goods, services, and information from origin to end customer. Manages procurement, warehousing, transportation, and demand forecasting.
required_skills: Supply chain planning and demand forecasting
Analytics and process improvement (Lean, Six Sigma)
education_required: BBA / B.Com / B.Tech (Engineering, especially industrial or mechanical)
MBA in Operations / Supply Chain Management
PG Diploma in Supply Chain Management (IIPM, IIMM, etc.)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: MBA in Operations/SCM from a top institution (IIM, NITIE, XLRI) is the preferred route for managerial roles. B.Tech Industrial Engineering also a strong pathway. APICS certifications are industry gold standards.
certifications: APICS CPIM (Certified in Production and Inventory Management)
entrance_exams: GATE (for NITIE, IIT Bombay M.Tech IEOR)
IIMM entrance exams (Indian Institute of Materials Management)
career_path_steps: Complete B.Tech or BBA/B.Com
Pursue MBA in Operations / Supply Chain or APICS CPIM certification
Start as Supply Chain Executive, Purchase Executive, or Logistics Coordinator
Learn SAP MM/WM and ERP usage
Advance to Manager → Senior Manager → AGM Supply Chain
Obtain APICS CSCP and Six Sigma certification
Lead cross-functional supply chain transformation projects
Progress to VP Operations → Supply Chain Director → COO
tools_or_technologies: SAP MM / SAP SCM / SAP S4HANA
careers.experience_level: Executive → Manager → Senior Manager → AGM → VP Operations → Supply Chain Director
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–7 LPA (entry); INR 10–20 LPA (manager, 4–6 years); INR 25–60 LPA (VP/Director level at large corporates/MNCs)
careers.future_scope: Supply chain resilience, near-shoring, India's PLI scheme driving manufacturing, e-commerce logistics boom, and cold chain infrastructure investment make this a high-growth domain.

careers.description: Designs studies, collects data, and applies statistical models to solve problems in medicine, public health, economics, government, and industry. Biostatisticians specialize in health/clinical trial data.
required_skills: Survival analysis and longitudinal data methods
education_required: B.Sc Statistics (Hons) or B.Sc Mathematics
M.Sc Statistics or Biostatistics
PhD Statistics or Biostatistics (for research and academic roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: M.Sc Statistics from ISI, IITK, DSE, or CMI is highly regarded. For biostatistics, M.Sc Public Health / Epidemiology with statistics focus at SCTIMST, AIIMS, or CMC Vellore is valued. PhD is required for academic and principal scientist positions.
entrance_exams: ISI Admission Test (for M.Stat at Indian Statistical Institute)
AIIMS and ICMR fellowship exams (for biostatistics research roles)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with Math background
Pursue B.Sc Statistics (Hons)
Clear ISI/IIT JAM for M.Sc Statistics
Complete M.Sc with focus on applied/biostatistics
Join pharmaceutical company, CROS, or research institution
Earn SAS programming certifications
Progress to Biostatistician → Senior Biostatistician → Lead Statistician
Pursue PhD for principal statistician or faculty roles
tools_or_technologies: CDISC standards (SDTM, ADaM — for clinical trials)
careers.experience_level: Junior Statistician / SAS Programmer → Statistician → Senior Statistician → Principal Statistician → Head of Biostatistics
careers.average_salary_range: INR 4–8 LPA (entry); INR 10–20 LPA (senior, 3–6 years); INR 20–45 LPA (principal/head in pharma/CRO); ISI faculty scale as per 7th CPC
careers.future_scope: Clinical trial growth in India, pharmaceutical statistics demand, healthcare analytics, and precision medicine are major drivers. AI/ML and statistics integration creates a new generation of data-driven statistical scientists.

careers.description: Operates medical imaging equipment including X-ray, CT, MRI, and ultrasound machines to produce diagnostic images for physicians. Ensures patient safety and image quality.
required_skills: Operation of radiological equipment (X-ray, CT, MRI, Ultrasound)
Anatomy knowledge for image interpretation context
education_required: 12th with PCB (preferred) or PCM
B.Sc Medical Imaging Technology / Radiography (3–4 years)
PG Diploma or M.Sc in Medical Imaging / Radiotherapy (for specialization)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: B.Sc Medical Imaging Technology from an AICTE/university-recognized college. AERB (Atomic Energy Regulatory Board) certification is required for operating radiation-producing equipment in India.
certifications: AERB Radiation Safety Certificate (mandatory for operating X-ray/CT/radiation therapy equipment)
ARRT Certification (American Registry of Radiologic Technologists — for international practice)
entrance_exams: NEET (some states use for B.Sc Paramedical admissions)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCB
Pursue B.Sc Medical Imaging Technology
Complete mandatory clinical internship
Obtain AERB radiation safety certification
Join hospital or diagnostic center as Radiographer
Specialize in CT, MRI, Mammography, or Interventional Radiology
Pursue M.Sc for senior technologist or academic roles
Advance to Senior Radiographer → Chief Radiographer → Imaging Head
careers.experience_level: Junior Radiographer → Radiographer → Senior Radiographer → Chief Radiographer → Quality Control Manager
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2.5–5 LPA (entry); INR 5–10 LPA (3–5 years, specialized modality); INR 10–20 LPA (senior/imaging head in private hospitals)
careers.future_scope: Growing with hospital expansion, diagnostic center chains (SRL, Lal Path Labs), tele-radiology, AI-powered image analysis, and increasing demand for CT/MRI diagnostics across India.

careers.description: Uses advanced mathematical and analytical methods such as optimization, simulation, and linear programming to help organizations make better decisions and improve operational efficiency.
required_skills: Python / R for optimization (PuLP, scipy.optimize, OR-Tools)
education_required: B.Tech in Industrial Engineering, Mathematics, or Computer Science
M.Sc Operations Research or M.Tech Industrial Engineering
MBA in Operations (quantitative focus)
PhD (for research-oriented positions)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: NITIE Mumbai, IIT Bombay (IEOR), IIT Delhi (DOMS/MBA), and ISI Kolkata are premier institutions for OR in India. M.Sc OR from Delhi University or M.Tech IE from NITs are standard pathways.
entrance_exams: IIT JAM Mathematics (for M.Sc OR at IITs)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with strong Math
Pursue B.Tech Industrial Engineering or B.Sc Mathematics
Learn Python/R for optimization and simulation
Pursue M.Tech IE or M.Sc OR from NITIE / IITs
Join as Operations Research Analyst or Data Analyst with OR focus
Work in consulting, logistics, manufacturing, or tech on optimization problems
Advance to Senior Analyst → OR Manager → Decision Science Director
careers.experience_level: Junior OR Analyst → OR Analyst → Senior Analyst → Lead Analyst → Decision Science Director
careers.average_salary_range: INR 5–10 LPA (entry); INR 12–25 LPA (3–6 years); INR 30–60 LPA (senior/consulting firm/tech firm lead)
careers.future_scope: Growing in AI-augmented optimization for logistics (Swiggy, Amazon, Flipkart), airline scheduling, financial portfolio optimization, healthcare resource allocation, and military operations research.

careers.description: Leads and manages the overall functioning of a school — including academic programs, staff management, student welfare, administrative operations, policy compliance, and community engagement.
required_skills: Vision setting and institutional culture building
education_required: B.Ed (mandatory)
M.Ed (Master of Education) — preferred or required for principal roles
MA/M.Sc in relevant subject + M.Ed
MBA in Education Management (alternate route)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: M.Ed from an NCTE-recognized institution is standard for principal eligibility in CBSE/state-affiliated schools. For CBSE schools, the principal must have M.Ed or equivalent with minimum years of teaching experience. Some IB/IGCSE schools accept international education management degrees.
certifications: National Initiative for School Heads (NISHTHA) Leadership Certification
School Leadership Programme — British Council / CBSE
entrance_exams: CUET-PG for M.Ed in central universities
School principal recruitment exams (KVS, NVS Principal Selection Test)
career_path_steps: Complete graduation in relevant subject
Pursue B.Ed from NCTE-recognized institution
Begin teaching career and clear CTET/State TET
Accumulate 5–10 years of teaching experience
Pursue M.Ed or MBA Education Management
Move to Vice Principal role
Advance to Principal position
Pursue KVS/NVS Principal examination for government schools
Contribute to curriculum, staff training, and school accreditation (NAAC, QCI)
tools_or_technologies: School ERP (School Management Systems: Fedena, Entab, MySchool)
careers.experience_level: Teacher → Senior Teacher → HOD → Vice Principal → Principal → Director of Education
careers.average_salary_range: INR 5–10 LPA (Vice Principal/private); INR 8–15 LPA (Principal private school); INR 6–12 LPA (KVS/NVS scale); INR 15–30 LPA (International school principal)
careers.future_scope: India's massive school system expansion, NEP 2020 implementation requiring leadership training, and growth of private and international schools ensure sustained demand for qualified educational leaders.

careers.description: Manages an organization's public image and communications. Crafts press releases, manages media relations, handles crisis communications, and builds brand reputation across stakeholders.
required_skills: Media relations and press release writing
Writing and editing for multiple audiences
Networking and relationship management with journalists
education_required: BA in Journalism, Mass Communication, or English
BA/B.Sc Public Relations (niche programs)
MA in Mass Communication or PR
MBA in Marketing / Communications (for senior corporate PR track)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: IIMC, Mudra Institute of Communications Ahmedabad (MICA), and Symbiosis Institute of Media and Communication (SIMC) are premier institutions. No single mandatory degree — portfolio of media work and networking are critical.
certifications: PRCA (Public Relations and Communications Association) Diploma
Google Analytics Certification (for digital PR metrics)
entrance_exams: CUET-PG for MA Mass Communication in central universities
career_path_steps: Complete 12th in any stream
Pursue BA Journalism / Mass Communication / English
Intern at a PR agency or corporate communications team
Build network of media contacts and portfolio of press releases
Join as PR Executive or Corporate Communications Coordinator
Advance to PR Manager → Senior PR Manager
Pursue MBA or PGDM for corporate communications leadership
Move to Head of Corporate Affairs → VP Communications → Chief Communications Officer (CCO)
careers.experience_level: PR Intern → PR Executive → Senior Executive → PR Manager → Head of Communications → CCO
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–6 LPA (entry); INR 8–15 LPA (manager, 3–5 years); INR 20–50 LPA (VP/CCO at large corporations)
careers.future_scope: Digital PR, influencer relations, ORM (online reputation management), and ESG communication are expanding the field. India's growing corporate ecosystem and startup ecosystem fuel demand for skilled PR professionals.

careers.description: A specialized CA who investigates financial fraud, embezzlement, money laundering, and corporate misconduct. Provides litigation support, expert witness testimony, and fraud prevention advisory.
required_skills: Understanding of IPC, PMLA, FEMA, and Income Tax Act
education_required: CA Final qualification (ICAI mandatory)
LLB (additional — strongly recommended for litigation support)
Forensic accounting diploma / postgraduate program
careers.specific_degree_requirements: CA membership (ACA/FCA) is the core credential. The ICAI Certificate Course on Forensic Accounting and Fraud Detection is the specialized overlay. CFE (Certified Fraud Examiner) is the international gold standard certification.
certifications: ICAI Certificate Course on Forensic Accounting and Fraud Detection
DISA (Diploma in Information Systems Audit) — ICAI
entrance_exams: ICAI CA Foundation / Intermediate / Final (primary pathway)
career_path_steps: Complete CA qualification (Foundation → Intermediate → Articleship → Final)
Gain 3–5 years of audit experience in public practice or industry
Pursue ICAI's Forensic Accounting certificate course
Earn Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE) designation
Join a forensic accounting firm or Big 4 Forensics division (EY Forensics, KPMG Forensics, etc.)
Handle fraud investigations, PMLA advisory, and litigation support
Advance to Senior Manager → Director Forensics → Independent Forensic Expert
tools_or_technologies: Python / R (for large dataset fraud analytics)
careers.experience_level: CA Fresher → Audit Manager → Forensic Analyst → Forensic Manager → Director Forensics → Independent Expert
careers.average_salary_range: INR 8–15 LPA (forensic analyst with CA); INR 20–45 LPA (senior manager); INR 50–100 LPA (Partner/Director at Big 4 Forensics); Independent CFE expert rates are very high
careers.future_scope: Growing demand from SFIO (Serious Fraud Investigation Office), ED, CBI, and courts for forensic accountants. Rising corporate fraud incidents, PMLA enforcement, and global anti-corruption initiatives are strong drivers.

careers.description: Diagnoses and treats diseases and injuries in animals. Works with companion animals, livestock, poultry, wildlife, and in public health, food safety, and zoonotic disease surveillance.
required_skills: Clinical examination and diagnosis of animals
Pharmacology and drug dosing for multiple species
education_required: 12th with PCB
BVSc & AH — Bachelor of Veterinary Science and Animal Husbandry (5.5 years including 1-year internship)
MVSc — Master of Veterinary Science (2 years, specialization — optional)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: Veterinary Council of India (VCI) registration is mandatory to practice. NEET-UG is required for admission to BVSc programs at most central and state veterinary colleges under MCI/VCI norms.
certifications: FNAAS (Fellow of National Academy of Agricultural Sciences — for senior academics)
entrance_exams: NEET-UG (for BVSc admission in most states)
State-level veterinary entrance exams (UPCPMT, KEAM for veterinary)
career_path_steps: Complete 12th with PCB
Clear NEET-UG / State Veterinary Entrance Exam
Enroll in BVSc & AH (5.5 years)
Complete compulsory rotating internship
Register with State Veterinary Council and VCI
Join as Clinical Veterinarian, Livestock Development Officer, or Government Vet
Pursue MVSc for specialization (Internal Medicine, Surgery, Pathology, Reproductive Biology)
Explore: ICAR research, FSSAI food safety, WHO/FAO animal health programs, or private small animal practice
Advance to Chief Veterinary Officer, Research Scientist, or Specialist Consultant
careers.experience_level: Intern Vet → Junior Vet / LDO → Senior Veterinarian → Chief Vet Officer → Research Scientist / Specialist
careers.average_salary_range: INR 3–7 LPA (government entry/private clinic); INR 8–15 LPA (senior govt/specialist); INR 15–35 LPA (specialist in metro private practice); ICAR/research roles per 7th CPC scales
careers.future_scope: Growing with increased pet ownership, animal husbandry modernization, zoonotic disease concerns (One Health approach), food safety regulations, and India's livestock export sector.

careers.description: Manages library resources, information systems, and knowledge services. Assists users in accessing, organizing, and retrieving information across physical and digital libraries.
required_skills: User education and information literacy programs
education_required: B.Lib.Sc (Bachelor of Library Science) or BLISc
M.Lib.Sc (Master of Library and Information Science) — preferred for senior/university library posts
PhD Library and Information Science (for academic and research librarian roles)
careers.specific_degree_requirements: M.Lib.Sc from recognized universities (TISS, BHU, Delhi University, DRTC Bangalore) is the minimum for university librarian positions. UGC-NET Library Science is required for appointment as university librarian under UGC norms.
certifications: UGC-NET Library and Information Science (for academic posts)
career_path_steps: Complete graduation in any discipline
Pursue B.Lib.Sc / BLISc
Pursue M.Lib.Sc / MLISc
Clear UGC-NET Library Science for eligibility for college/university positions
Start as Library Assistant → Junior Librarian
Build digital library and cataloguing skills
Apply for School Librarian (KVS/NVS), College Librarian, or Corporate Information Center roles
Advance to Senior Librarian → Deputy Librarian → Chief Librarian
Pursue PhD for Deputy/Chief Librarian at universities or academic posts
careers.experience_level: Library Trainee → Junior Librarian → Librarian → Senior Librarian → Deputy Librarian → Chief Librarian
careers.average_salary_range: INR 2.5–5 LPA (school/small college); INR 5–10 LPA (university/government, 7th CPC); INR 8–18 LPA (corporate information centers / IIM / IIT libraries)
careers.future_scope: Shift toward digital libraries, research data management, institutional repositories, and information architecture is creating new opportunities. Government libraries (Parliament, SC) and DRDO/ISRO archives offer specialized roles.
